documents.title: Address Before the Young Men's Lyceum of Springfield, Illinois
documents.source_citation: Project Gutenberg EBook #3253 — The Papers and Writings of Abraham Lincoln, Complete, ed. Arthur Brooks Lapsley. Date note: Lapsley edition reads '1837'; correct date is 1838-01-27 per Basler, Collected Works vol. 1, p. 108.
documents.source_edition: Project Gutenberg EBook #3253, extracted from The Papers and Writings of Abraham Lincoln, Complete, Constitutional Edition, ed. Arthur Brooks Lapsley
documents.editorial_status: public-domain transcription with Gutenberg header/footer stripped; Lapsley date error retained in body but corrected in metadata
documents.editorial_notes: The raw body prints January 27, 1837 following the Lapsley/Gutenberg source. Project metadata uses 1838-01-27, following Basler, Collected Works vol. 1, p. 108.
documents.inclusion_rationale: Early benchmark for Lincoln on political institutions, inherited republican obligation, law, mob violence, and the transition from revolutionary memory to civic reverence.
documents.structural_encoding: single body section segmented into stable paragraph, sentence, and lexical-unit identifiers
documents.known_limitations: Gutenberg/Lapsley transcription is adequate for pilot annotation but should be cross-checked against Basler before publication-level quotation.

documents.title: First Inaugural Address
documents.source_citation: Lincoln, Abraham. Abraham Lincoln's First Inaugural Address. Project Gutenberg EBook #9.
documents.source_edition: Project Gutenberg EBook #9, standalone First Inaugural transcription
documents.editorial_status: public-domain transcription with Gutenberg header/footer stripped
documents.inclusion_rationale: Adds immediate secession-crisis evidence for Union, constitutional oath, force limits, federal property, minority rule, and sacred civic bonds before battlefield sacrifice.
documents.structural_encoding: single body section segmented into stable paragraph, sentence, and lexical-unit identifiers
documents.known_limitations: Public-domain transcription is adequate for draft annotation; exact wording should be cross-checked against LOC or Collected Works before publication-level quotation.

documents.title: Message to Congress in Special Session, July 4, 1861
documents.source_citation: Lincoln, Abraham. The Papers and Writings of Abraham Lincoln, Volume 5: 1858-1862. Project Gutenberg EBook #2657.

required_phrases: The Union is older than any of the States

documents.source_edition: Project Gutenberg EBook #2657, extracted from The Papers and Writings of Abraham Lincoln, Volume 5
documents.editorial_status: public-domain transcription with Gutenberg header/footer stripped and unrelated volume content omitted
documents.inclusion_rationale: Adds war-authorization and democracy-as-experiment evidence: rebellion, constitutional crisis, Union continuity, sacrifice, and early-war public justification.
documents.structural_encoding: single body section segmented into stable paragraph, sentence, and lexical-unit identifiers
documents.known_limitations: Long presidential message increases annotation burden; exact wording should be cross-checked against LOC or Collected Works before publication-level quotation.

documents.title: Address Delivered at the Dedication of the Cemetery at Gettysburg
documents.source_citation: Project Gutenberg EBook #4 — Bliss copy (Lincoln's final corrected manuscript, the only one he signed)

required_phrases: Four score and seven years ago

documents.source_edition: Project Gutenberg EBook #4, Bliss copy transcription
documents.editorial_status: public-domain Bliss copy transcription with Gutenberg header/footer stripped
documents.editorial_notes: Gutenberg represents pauses with spaced ellipses; the Bliss manuscript does not use ellipses in the same way. Manuscript/version choice is analytically important and should be noted in publication.
documents.inclusion_rationale: Canonical sacrificial-economy text for battlefield death, national survival, dedication, consecration, unfinished work, and new birth of freedom.
documents.structural_encoding: single body section segmented into stable paragraph, sentence, and lexical-unit identifiers
documents.known_limitations: The Bliss copy is used for pilot consistency; variant manuscript wording should be checked before claims depend on exact phrasing.

documents.title: Second Inaugural Address
documents.source_citation: Project Gutenberg EBook #8 — Abraham Lincoln's Second Inaugural Address
documents.source_edition: Project Gutenberg EBook #8, Abraham Lincoln's Second Inaugural Address
documents.editorial_status: public-domain transcription with Gutenberg header/footer stripped
documents.editorial_notes: Early Gutenberg transcription uses older formatting conventions. Cross-check against Library of Congress manuscript before publication-level quotation.
documents.inclusion_rationale: Central final-war text for Providence, oath, Union, shared guilt, blood repayment, judgment, reconciliation, and healing language.
documents.structural_encoding: single body section segmented into stable paragraph, sentence, and lexical-unit identifiers
documents.known_limitations: Reliable for pilot annotation; manuscript cross-check remains required before publication-level quotation.
